mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers discount rates and supports equity multiples, so a sustained easing impulse predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX forward returns are not positive in at least 55% of months with a 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or if the rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in UNRATE above a recent threshold signals labor-market deterioration, weaker consumption, and lower earnings expectations, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of cases, or if the signal's hit rate is indistinguishable from 50% at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and PAYEMS both accelerate together, household spending and payroll income are improving at the same time, signaling broad demand strength that supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition does not outperform either proxy alone, or if 20-day SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of months when both series are rising on a 3-month basis.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained acceleration in INDPRO indicates improving industrial activity and operating leverage, which should feed into better earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of instances after a two-month rise in INDPRO, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC remains below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in HOUST after a prior contraction signals stabilizing housing demand and wealth effects, which should improve cyclical sentiment and lift S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises by more than 5% from its prior 3-month low
failure_condition: Reject if the rebound condition is followed by negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of cases, or if the average forward return is not above the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation is rising while FEDFUNDS remains near the zero lower bound, real rates stay deeply negative and financial conditions remain supportive, which should favor positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < 0.5% and CPIAUCSL rises on a 3-month annualized basis
failure_condition: Reject if the joint low-rate/high-inflation condition does not produce positive 5-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the interaction term is not significant in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent increase in PAYEMS indicates broad labor-income growth and stronger consumer demand, which should translate into higher S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day SPX forward returns are not positive in at least 55% of periods when PAYEMS rises for three straight months, or if the signal reverses in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous slowdown in GDPC1 and RSAFS signals that final demand is weakening across both output and sales, which should pressure earnings expectations and lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 and RSAFS both decline versus their prior observation
failure_condition: Reject if joint declines in GDPC1 and RSAFS are not followed by negative 60-day SPX returns more than 50% of the time, or if either series alone explains the effect equally well.
